steps.title: Make a one-row value table
steps.content: The value $k$ means $f(1)$. In Desmos, make one table row with $x_1=[1]$, then evaluate each answer choice at that same input. Since the choices are equivalent forms, they should all output the same value; the goal is to find which one displays that value as the coefficient or base.

steps.title: Find the form where the exponent becomes 0
steps.content: The table shows $f(1)=98$. The coefficient shows $k$ directly only when the exponential factor becomes $1$. Choice C has $x-1$ in the exponent, so at $x=1$ it gives $98(1.4)^0=98$. The coefficient $98$ is exactly $k$.

steps.title: Eliminate the shifted forms
steps.content: Choice A shows the value at $x=-1$, choice B shows the value at $x=0$, and choice D shows the value at $x=2$. Only choice C shows $f(1)$ as the coefficient. Choice C is correct.
